title: 1: Code Maintainability
1.1: Adds fil_space_t::defragment() method for system tablespace defragmentation
1.2: Performs each page move atomically within a single mini-transaction
1.3: Restores original page state on failure during page relocation
1.4: Updates all 3 B-tree references (parent child pointer, left sibling next pointer, right sibling previous pointer) when relocating a page
1.5: Invokes defragmentation during server restart, not shutdown
1.6: Skips defragmentation when user tables exist in system tablespace
1.7: Defragments all 7 system dictionary tables (SYS_TABLES, SYS_COLUMNS, SYS_INDEXES, SYS_FIELDS, SYS_FOREIGN, SYS_FOREIGN_COLS, SYS_VIRTUAL)
1.8: Modifies flst_validate() to return dberr_t error codes instead of using assertions
1.9: Makes flst_validate() available in release builds by removing debug-only guards
1.10: Calls fsp_tablespace_validate() to validate tablespace free-space lists during the defragmentation flow
1.11: Validates index segment inode lists before and after defragmentation operations
2: Documentation Maintainability
3: Artifact Cleanup
3.1: Refactors xdes_get_state() to use uint32_t with combined declaration-assignment for the state variable
3.2: Tracks extent state to identify free and used extents
3.3: Traverses B-tree levels to relocate pages
3.4: Adds dict_sys_t::is_sys_table() to distinguish system tables from user tables
3.5: Implements atomic list manipulation for extent descriptor operations
4: Negative Rubrics
4.1: Breaks existing tablespace truncation logic in fsp_system_tablespace_truncate()
